channel.title: Feeds Student Journalism and feeds about Journalism feeds

item.title: Gabriel Clement II to see homecoming at track and field’s Battle on the Bayou
item.description: Homecomings often elicit a wide range of emotions. And Gabriel Clement II said he is embracing them all. He is heading home. The junior sprinter will compete less than 100 miles away from his hometown of Kaplan, Louisiana, on Friday and Saturday at the Battle on the Bayou in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, where his family will cheer him on.
item.source:markdown: Homecomings often elicit a wide range of emotions. And Gabriel Clement II said he is embracing them all. He is heading home. The junior sprinter will compete less than 100 miles away from his hometown of Kaplan, Louisiana, on Friday and Saturday at the Battle on the Bayou in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, where his family will cheer him on.

item.title: Film review: ‘Project Hail Mary’ is a stellar adaptation worth watching
item.description: Warning: spoilers ahead. All hail “Project Hail Mary,” a faithful and stellar adaptation of its source material. Derived from Andy Weir’s 2021 science fiction novel of the same name, “Project Hail Mary” from directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller landed in theaters on March 20.
item.source:markdown: Warning: spoilers ahead. All hail “Project Hail Mary,” a faithful and stellar adaptation of its source material. Derived from Andy Weir’s 2021 science fiction novel of the same name, “Project Hail Mary” from directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller landed in theaters on March 20.

item.title: NADC Burger’s taste, quality fails to make up for its high price point
item.description: NADC Burger proves to be nothing more than an overhyped patty, with a price tag richer than its flavor profile. The highly anticipated burger restaurant arrived in Westwood with the swagger of a must-try classic.

item.title: UCLA track and field splits for weekend, sends athletes to Baton Rouge, Palo Alto
item.description: Home is not always on the schedule for college athletes. But for one Bruin, this weekend’s meet will offer exactly that. UCLA track and field will divide its roster across two competitions, sending nine distance runners to compete at the Stanford Invite in Palo Alto, California while sprinters and field athletes will head to compete in the Battle on the Bayou meet in Baton Rouge, Louisiana.
item.source:markdown: Home is not always on the schedule for college athletes. But for one Bruin, this weekend’s meet will offer exactly that. UCLA track and field will divide its roster across two competitions, sending nine distance runners to compete at the Stanford Invite in Palo Alto, California while sprinters and field athletes will head to compete in the Battle on the Bayou meet in Baton Rouge, Louisiana.

item.title: The Provincetown Independent’s reporters couldn’t find housing. So the Local Journalism Project bought a condo for them to rent.
item.description: Paying the rent on a reporter’s salary isn’t easy anywhere these days. But on the Outer Cape, it’s almost impossible. Massachusetts has some of the highest housing costs in the country. The problem is exacerbated on Cape Cod’s Outer Cape, a region that includes Provincetown, Truro, Wellfleet, and Eastham. In a community where the same...

item.title: UCLA baseball faces crosstown rivals USC on brink of program straight wins record
item.description: Twenty-straight wins. No other team in the nation has more than nine. Just two victories shy of matching the program record of 22 straight – set in 2010 by a team that featured future major leaguers Gerrit Cole and Trevor Bauer – the Bruins could make history this weekend.
item.source:markdown: Twenty-straight wins. No other team in the nation has more than nine. Just two victories shy of matching the program record of 22 straight – set in 2010 by a team that featured future major leaguers Gerrit Cole and Trevor Bauer – the Bruins could make history this weekend.

item.title: Amid internal uncertainty, the VTDigger’s new union contract guarantees journalists’ input on AI use
item.description: After a year of negotiating, the VTDigger Guild ratified its second-ever union contract on April 1 with VTDigger, the nonprofit news outlet covering Vermont. The new four-year agreement guarantees a 32.5% increase to the minimum salary for reporters, more paid time off, and journalists’ input on the use of artificial intelligence. Here’s what the contract...

item.description: With “Spamalot,” it’s unclear if the Holy Grail is half-full or half-empty. To the sound of clanking coconuts, the Tony Award-winning musical comedy galloped into the Hollywood Pantages Theatre on March 24 for a tour stop lasting until April 12.

item.title: UCLA gymnastics 2026 postseason predictions
item.description: No. 4 seed UCLA gymnastics is postseason bound and will open regional competition Friday in Corvallis, Oregon. The Bruins must advance through quad meets – with the top two teams reaching Sunday’s regional final – and the top two there earning a trip to Fort Worth, Texas for the championships.
item.source:markdown: No. 4 seed UCLA gymnastics is postseason bound and will open regional competition Friday in Corvallis, Oregon. The Bruins must advance through quad meets – with the top two teams reaching Sunday’s regional final – and the top two there earning a trip to Fort Worth, Texas for the championships.

item.title: UCLA researchers develop cell therapy for aggressive breast cancer treatment
item.description: This post was updated March 31 at 11:21 p.m. UCLA researchers developed a human stem cell-derived cell therapy for an aggressive type of breast cancer. This study focused on modifying natural killer T – or NKT – cells, which are a specialized immune cell that quickly responds to cancer cells, according to the National Cancer Institute.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated March 31 at 11:21 p.m._ UCLA researchers developed a human stem cell-derived cell therapy for an aggressive type of breast cancer. This study focused on modifying natural killer T – or NKT – cells, which are a specialized immune cell that quickly responds to cancer cells, according to the National Cancer Institute.

item.title: The ‘Bruin Bombers’ lay the foundation for UCLA’s hitting success
item.description: One superstar helps a team win games. Two make victories feel inevitable. As No. 8 UCLA Softball (28-3, Big Ten 9-0) rides a 22-game winning streak, much of the team’s success can be attributed to the dominant performance of the “Bruin Bombers.” Seniors utility Megan Grant and infielder Jordan Woolery have been on an offensive tear this season, combining for 42 home runs and a .542 batting average.
item.source:markdown: One superstar helps a team win games. Two make victories feel inevitable. As No. 8 UCLA Softball (28-3, Big Ten 9-0) rides a 22-game winning streak, much of the team’s success can be attributed to the dominant performance of the “Bruin Bombers.” Seniors utility Megan Grant and infielder Jordan Woolery have been on an offensive tear this season, combining for 42 home runs and a .542 batting average.

item.title: Highland Park Coffee Passport travels beyond coffee

item.title: Committee recommends removal of Chávez’s name from Chicana/o studies department
item.description: UCLA’s Honorific Naming Committee unanimously recommended the removal of Cesár E. Chávez’s name from the department of Chicana/o and Central American Studies on Friday, following a Wednesday New York Times investigation which revealed his alleged sex abuse.  The committee – which received the request from the department Wednesday – sent its recommendation to Chancellor Julio Frenk and Executive Vice Chancellor and Provost Darnell Hunt, according to a Friday letter obtained by the Daily Bruin.
item.source:markdown: UCLA’s Honorific Naming Committee unanimously recommended the removal of Cesár E. Chávez’s name from the department of Chicana/o and Central American Studies on Friday, following a Wednesday New York Times investigation which revealed his alleged sex abuse. item.title: United Auto Workers units finalize agreements with UC, avoid strike
item.description: Three United Auto Workers units ratified contracts with the UC on Friday, averting a strike after months of bargaining.  The three units – UAW Local 4811’s academic student employees unit, Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW and Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW – represent more than 40,000 UC academic and research employees, including teaching assistants.
item.source:markdown: Three United Auto Workers units ratified contracts with the UC on Friday, averting a strike after months of bargaining. The three units – UAW Local 4811’s academic student employees unit, Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW and Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW – represent more than 40,000 UC academic and research employees, including teaching assistants.

item.title: Attending UCLA’s School of Dentistry is going to get more expensive
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO – The UC Board of Regents approved a tuition increase for UCLA’s dentistry program at its Wednesday meeting. The academic and student affairs committee approved the tuition increase for the dentistry program – as well as for a nursing program at UC Irvine and pharmacy programs at UCI and UC San Francisco – at its bimonthly meeting, held Tuesday and Wednesday at UCSF.
item.source:markdown: SAN FRANCISCO – The UC Board of Regents approved a tuition increase for UCLA’s dentistry program at its Wednesday meeting. The academic and student affairs committee approved the tuition increase for the dentistry program – as well as for a nursing program at UC Irvine and pharmacy programs at UCI and UC San Francisco – at its bimonthly meeting, held Tuesday and Wednesday at UCSF.

item.title: UCLA men’s tennis looks to extend win streak in Wisconsin, Nebraska matchups
item.description: Rivalries bring intensity. But once the matchups are in the rearview, teams look to carry that energy forward. No. 24 UCLA men’s tennis (9-3, 3-0 Big Ten) will have the chance to do just that as it prepares to face Wisconsin (11-3, 2-2) on Friday and Nebraska (9-6, 1-3) on Sunday at the Los Angeles Tennis Center.
item.source:markdown: Rivalries bring intensity. But once the matchups are in the rearview, teams look to carry that energy forward. item.title: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok are all bad at crediting news outlets, but ChatGPT is the worst (at least in this study)
item.description: Canadian researchers asked the paid and free or “economy” versions of four AI models — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok — about Canadian news events to see whether they would credit individual news outlets in their answers. The answer will probably not surprise you: AI models rarely cite news sources unless they’re specifically asked to,...

item.title: UC approves $298.2 million budget to convert LA building into UCLA Health complex
item.description: The UC Board of Regents approved a $298.2 million budget Wednesday to convert a Los Angeles building into a UCLA Health complex. The project will convert an office building in El Segundo into UCLA Health South Bay, which will house a Sports Medicine Institute and create community fill and specialty pharmacies.
item.source:markdown: The UC Board of Regents approved a $298.2 million budget Wednesday to convert a Los Angeles building into a UCLA Health complex. The project will convert an office building in El Segundo into UCLA Health South Bay, which will house a Sports Medicine Institute and create community fill and specialty pharmacies.

item.title: Bruin baseball holds on, wins against the Waves 5-4 at Jackie Robinson Stadium
item.description: The song played in ballparks across the country may be failing to capture a key part of baseball. “Take Me Out to the Ballgame” ends with, “For it’s one, two, three strikes you’re out, / At the old ballgame.” But batters typically get more than a single plate appearance.

item.description:

item.title: A Letter to Future Generations: On the Philosophy of Happiness — Part V
item.description: Okay, so I’ve thrown a lot of philosophy at you. But here’s the thing: this isn’t meant to be intellectual trivia for impressing people at parties. The ancient Greeks called philosophy a “way of life” — not an academic subject, but a set of practices for living well.
item.source:markdown: Okay, so I’ve thrown a lot of philosophy at you. But here’s the thing: this isn’t meant to be intellectual trivia for impressing people at parties. The ancient Greeks called philosophy a “way of life” — not an academic subject, but a set of practices for living well.

item.title: Beavers Have Ice in Their Veins: Caltech Winter ‘25-26 Athletics
item.description: As many of us know, s’more year fall and winter terms are absolute killers. Unfortunately, I was also a victim of this, which is why instead of my usual periodic updates throughout the winter athletic season, I am bringing to you all a complete season recap in a nutshell!
item.source:markdown: As many of us know, s’more year fall and winter terms are absolute killers. Unfortunately, I was also a victim of this, which is why instead of my usual periodic updates throughout the winter athletic season, I am bringing to you all a complete season recap in a nutshell!

item.title: Caltech Astrophysicist Carl Grillmair Dies at 67
item.description: Carl Grillmair, an astronomer at Caltech’s IPAC science and data center for astronomy and planetary science, died on February 16. He was 67. His death has shaken the Caltech community, where colleagues remember as both a creative scientist and a foundational member of IPAC’s research programs.
item.source:markdown: Carl Grillmair, an astronomer at Caltech’s IPAC science and data center for astronomy and planetary science, died on February 16. He was 67. His death has shaken the Caltech community, where colleagues remember as both a creative scientist and a foundational member of IPAC’s research programs.

item.title: Caltech Wildlife: Hummingbirds
item.description: Did you know that hummingbirds are a family of birds found exclusively in the Western Hemisphere (here chants the Bad Bunny version listing all American countries)? When I first arrived at Caltech, I found these hovering drones such a blessed sight.
item.source:markdown: Did you know that hummingbirds are a family of birds found exclusively in the Western Hemisphere (here chants the Bad Bunny version listing all American countries)? When I first arrived at Caltech, I found these hovering drones such a blessed sight.

item.title: Congress Votes to Retain Science Funding, Declines Most Proposed Cuts
item.description: On March 4, the Senate Commerce Committee voted to advance the bipartisan NASA Authorization Act of 2026, authorizing a $24.7 billion budget for this fiscal year, extending operations for the International Space Station through 2032, and — for the first time — backing plans for a permanent crewed presence on the Moon.
item.source:markdown: On March 4, the Senate Commerce Committee voted to advance the bipartisan NASA Authorization Act of 2026, authorizing a $24.7 billion budget for this fiscal year, extending operations for the International Space Station through 2032, and — for the first time — backing plans for a permanent crewed presence on the Moon.

item.title: Dabney Hosts Lunar New Year-Themed Interhovse
item.description: On the night of February 21, Dabney Hovse welcomed students across campus for its Lunar New Year-themed Interhovse celebration. The courtyard centerpiece was a striking red dome topped with gold, glowing from within with shifting colored lights that illuminated the space throughout the night.
item.source:markdown: On the night of February 21, Dabney Hovse welcomed students across campus for its Lunar New Year-themed Interhovse celebration. The courtyard centerpiece was a striking red dome topped with gold, glowing from within with shifting colored lights that illuminated the space throughout the night.

item.title: Dimming the Sun: Can a Satellite Fleet Buy Earth the Time It Needs?
item.description: When I first met Pedro Neves, a visiting researcher from NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, it was at a Watson Lecture in October 2025. Our shared native language, Portuguese, provided an immediate shorthand, but the conversation quickly moved to the mechanics of the Sun-Earth L1 Lagrange point.
item.source:markdown: When I first met Pedro Neves, a visiting researcher from NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, it was at a Watson Lecture in October 2025. Our shared native language, Portuguese, provided an immediate shorthand, but the conversation quickly moved to the mechanics of the Sun-Earth L1 Lagrange point.

item.title: Stop Trying to Convert Climate Deniers: A Psychologist’s Guide to Actually Making a Difference
item.description: When you see an event titled “Communicating The Climate Crisis,” you might expect another earnest lecture about melting ice caps accompanied by guilt-inducing polar bear photos. But Wändi Bruine de Bruin — Provost Professor of Public Policy, Psychology and Behavioral Science at USC — had a different message for the Student Activism Speaker Series crowd: You’re doing it wrong.
item.source:markdown: When you see an event titled “Communicating The Climate Crisis,” you might expect another earnest lecture about melting ice caps accompanied by guilt-inducing polar bear photos. But Wändi Bruine de Bruin — Provost Professor of Public Policy, Psychology and Behavioral Science at USC — had a different message for the Student Activism Speaker Series crowd: You’re doing it wrong.

item.title: The Eyes That Search for Tomorrow — Part II
item.description: 7:09 PM. The Walk Home. The heat hits me like a wall when I step outside. The medical center is still busy, but I feel like I’m moving through a different dimension than everyone else.
item.source:markdown: 7:09 PM. The Walk Home. The heat hits me like a wall when I step outside. The medical center is still busy, but I feel like I’m moving through a different dimension than everyone else.

item.title: The Friendships That Introduced European Modernism to California
item.description: In 1915, Emilie Esther Scheyer came across The Hunchback (1911) by artist Alexei Jawlensky in Lausanne, Switzerland. The painting was so impactful that she was determined to meet Jawlensky, setting the course for her life and career, as well as the development of modern art in California.
item.source:markdown: In 1915, Emilie Esther Scheyer came across _The Hunchback_ (1911) by artist Alexei Jawlensky in Lausanne, Switzerland. The painting was so impactful that she was determined to meet Jawlensky, setting the course for her life and career, as well as the development of modern art in California.

item.title: The Transcendental Return of Pi Day
item.description: Thanks to the Interhouse Committee, Caltech’s Pi Day tradition rose again this year — a very Caltech study break for a very Caltech finals season. The well-rounded celebration happened right on schedule at 1:59 a.m. on March 14, where night-owl pi(e) enthusiasts converged in the Ricketts-Fleming courtyard for an event that was, by all measures, irrationally extravagant.
item.source:markdown: Thanks to the Interhouse Committee, Caltech’s Pi Day tradition rose again this year — a very Caltech study break for a very Caltech finals season. The well-rounded celebration happened right on schedule at 1:59 a.m. on March 14, where night-owl pi(e) enthusiasts converged in the Ricketts-Fleming courtyard for an event that was, by all measures, irrationally extravagant.

item.title: Kiran Desai expands on new book’s theme of loneliness at Hammer Museum talk
item.description: Kiran Desai explored loneliness and connection across space and time at the Hammer Museum on Thursday. Desai – the Booker Prize and National Book Critics Circle Award-winning author of “Hullabaloo in the Guava Orchard” and “The Inheritance of Loss” – spoke with UCLA professor Mona Simpson in this year’s final iteration of Simpson’s “Some Favorite Writers” series.
item.source:markdown: Kiran Desai explored loneliness and connection across space and time at the Hammer Museum on Thursday. Desai – the Booker Prize and National Book Critics Circle Award-winning author of “Hullabaloo in the Guava Orchard” and “The Inheritance of Loss” – spoke with UCLA professor Mona Simpson in this year’s final iteration of Simpson’s “Some Favorite Writers” series.

item.title: UC reaches tentative agreements with 3 United Auto Workers units
item.description: The UC reached tentative agreements with three United Auto Workers units that had threatened to strike, the units announced Friday. The tentative agreement, if approved, will become the first contracts for Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW and Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW, which were officially recognized by the University in April and September, respectively.
item.source:markdown: The UC reached tentative agreements with three United Auto Workers units that had threatened to strike, the units announced Friday. The tentative agreement, if approved, will become the first contracts for Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW and Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW, which were officially recognized by the University in April and September, respectively.

item.title: Eric Dailey Jr. talks the talk, walks the walk as men’s basketball’s vocal leader
item.description: Eric Dailey Jr. scored zero points in UCLA’s 82-80 victory against Washington on Dec. 3. He simmered on the plane ride from Seattle back to Los Angeles.

item.title: Art History Undergraduate Student Association blends learning, community-building
item.description: UCLA’s Art History Undergraduate Student Association is painting a colorful community for art history lovers. AHUSA is a student-led club dedicated to exploring art history through discussion, faculty speakers and gallery visits.
item.source:markdown: UCLA’s Art History Undergraduate Student Association is painting a colorful community for art history lovers. AHUSA is a student-led club dedicated to exploring art history through discussion, faculty speakers and gallery visits.

item.title: ‘ICE Out: Arte en Resistencia’ exhibit demonstrates power of speech in art
item.description: Some exhibits aren’t made for looking at, but rather for looking back. On Tuesday, the UCLA Chicano Studies Research Center Library transformed into a gallery. The Center for the Study of International Migration and the Chicano Studies Research Center Library filled the space with murals, paintings, photography, music and a community that showed up to heal.
item.source:markdown: Some exhibits aren’t made for looking at, but rather for looking back. On Tuesday, the UCLA Chicano Studies Research Center Library transformed into a gallery. The Center for the Study of International Migration and the Chicano Studies Research Center Library filled the space with murals, paintings, photography, music and a community that showed up to heal.

item.title: Five Things: With a well-bonded team and bolstered staff, UCLA works toward a MCWS appearance
item.description: Ahead of No. 1 UCLA baseball’s (14-2, 3-0 Big Ten) weekend series with Michigan (9-6) at Jackie Robinson Stadium, assistant Sports editor Gabriela Garcia shares five thoughts on the Bruins’ first 16 games of the 2026 campaign, as the squad rides an eight-game win streak and its best start since 2010.

item.title: UCLA takes first-ever Big Ten tournament victory with Donovan Dent’s triple-double
item.description: This post was updated March 12 at 10:59 p.m. CHICAGO – The alarm clock rang. It was only 6 p.m. on the West Coast when tip-off commenced, but the Bruins still seemed deep in their slumber.

item.title: UC UAW units may strike ‘as soon as next week’ if University doesn’t meet demands
item.description: Union members representing UC academic and research employees said at a demonstration Thursday that they plan to strike as soon as next week if the University does not meet their contract demands.
item.source:markdown: Union members representing UC academic and research employees said at a demonstration Thursday that they plan to strike as soon as next week if the University does not meet their contract demands.
